name: Devoted to Destruction

verdict.issue: The rule fires only when the model is equipped with 2 Telemon caestus weapons in addition to its armoured feet weapon. The authored DSL applies the +2 Attacks modifier unconditionally — no equipment-loadout condition is present. A stated condition is missing.

reasoning: The +2 A modifier is narrowed to the named weapon via weapon_name, fixing the draft's omission of weapon specificity. The equip precondition (exactly 2 caestus) has no representable condition type in the grammar; because loadout is fixed at game-start the passive is equivalent — the ability is simply absent from any model that doesn't carry two caestus. The 'in addition to armoured feet' clause is a description of the legal load-out, not a separate runtime condition.

name: Dread Foe

verdict.issue: The rule uses a single D6 with +2 added if charged, then checks thresholds on that one result (4-5 → D3 MW, 6+ → 3 MW). The authored DSL nests a second dice-gated node — also specifying "dice":"D6" — inside the on_fail branch of each conditional arm. That explicit second dice specification implies rolling a fresh D6 rather than checking a lower range on the same die, so the model produces two dice rolls where one is required. The threshold values themselves are correctly adjusted for the charge bonus, but the single-roll multi-threshold mechanic is structurally misrepresented.

reasoning: The +2 charge bonus shifts raw-roll breakpoints: without charge the 6+ gate sits at threshold 6 and the 4-5 gate at threshold 4; with +2 the same result-6 threshold is reached at raw roll 4, and result-4 at raw roll 2. The two branches are mutually exclusive conditionals in a sequence (conditional has no else clause). Nested dice-gated on_fail models the 4-5 vs 6+ split on the same conceptual roll — the outer node catches the higher band, the inner node catches the lower band on the same roll's failure path.

name: Unyielding Ancient

verdict.issue: The rule applies only the first time this model is destroyed — a once-per-battle trigger. The authored DSL contains no "once" qualifier or first-time condition, meaning the suppress-deadly-demise + resurrection sequence would trigger every time the model is destroyed. A stated limiting condition is absent.

reasoning: The sequence encodes three distinct sub-effects the draft collapsed: (1) suppression of Deadly Demise, modelled as an ability-grant with grant_type suppress-deadly-demise since no dedicated suppress leaf exists; (2) the end-of-phase 2+ resurrection gate via dice-gated; (3) the D6 wounds restoration on a successful resurrection via the wounds_remaining key on the resurrection modifier. Scope duration battle captures the once-per-battle 'first time destroyed' restriction. The wounds_remaining key is not listed in the canonical modifier table but is the most precisely named key for setting remaining wound count on revival.

name: Leader

verdict.issue: Core mechanic (CHARACTER attaches to a Bodyguard unit) is correctly captured by the bodyguard-attachment grant. The "before the battle" pregame timing is not modelled — the DSL has no pre-game trigger, only a battle-duration scope — which is a secondary detail imperfectly represented.

reasoning: The rule describes a pre-battle setup procedure (attach a CHARACTER to a Bodyguard unit before the battle begins), not an in-battle effect. This falls squarely in the meta-procedure category the spec designates unencodable. The best partial encoding captures that this ability grants the right to form a bodyguard attachment, but the pre-battle timing, the CHARACTER/Bodyguard pairing constraint, and the unit-formation mechanism are all outside the in-battle effect grammar.

name: Advanced Firepower

verdict.issue: Both weapon/target pairings are correctly modelled: accelerator cannon gets Lethal Hits vs non-MONSTER/non-VEHICLE, blaze cannon gets Lethal Hits vs MONSTER/VEHICLE. Conditions, weapon names, and keyword grants are all accurate.

reasoning: The two weapons have complementary targeting conditions: iliastus gets Lethal Hits when the target is NOT (MONSTER or VEHICLE), arachnus gets it when the target IS (MONSTER or VEHICLE). Each is a separate conditional keyword-grant narrowed by weapon_name; per-attack target-has-keyword conditions gate which grant fires. The compound not-or condition correctly encodes the 'excluding MONSTERS and VEHICLES' clause for the iliastus arm.

name: Slayers of Tyrants

verdict.issue: The condition (targeting CHARACTER, MONSTER, or VEHICLE) and the wound re-roll are correct. "subset":"all-failures" models the re-roll as mandatory on every failure, whereas the rule says "you can re-roll" (player's choice). The optionality is not captured, though for wound rolls this is practically equivalent.

reasoning: The draft applied the re-roll unconditionally; the fix wraps it in a target-keyword OR gate across all three eligible target types (CHARACTER, MONSTER, VEHICLE). subset all-failures correctly models 'you can re-roll the Wound roll' — 10th edition re-roll permission covers failed rolls, not just 1s. target unit is correct because the rule applies to every model in the unit, not just the bearer.

name: From Golden Light

verdict.issue: Condition stack (end-of-turn, opponent turn, not in Engagement Range), deep-strike effect, and battle duration all correctly model the rule.

reasoning: End-of-opponent's-turn trigger encoded as timing-is(end-of-turn) AND player-turn-is(opponent); engagement-range exclusion as not(engagement-state); Strategic Reserves removal+redeploy as deep-strike (parameterless); battle duration captures once-per-battle frequency.

name: Sweeping Advance

verdict.issue: The in-Engagement-Range branch uses 'fallback-and-act', which is the named 40K mechanic granting Fall Back plus the ability to still shoot/charge. The rule grants only a plain Fall Back move. This is a core mechanic overstep.

reasoning: Outer condition gates on end-of-phase timing + phase-is(fight) + has-fought-this-phase. Inner sequence contains two mutually exclusive engagement-state conditionals: engaged path grants fallback-and-act, not-engaged path grants a normal movement; battle duration captures once-per-battle frequency. Sequence (not choice) is correct because game state determines which branch applies, not player preference.

name: Daughters of the Abyss

verdict.issue: Feel No Pain 3+ conditioned on psychic OR mortal-wound, scoped to unit/battle. Correctly models the rule.

reasoning: FNP 3+ is gated by an OR compound: attack-is-type(psychic) covers Psychic Attacks; attack-is-type(mortal-wound) covers mortal wounds. Without this wrapper the FNP would apply universally, which the rule does not permit.

name: Saturation Volleys

verdict.issue: Core mechanic (post-shot trigger, hit-check ≥1, MONSTER/VEHICLE exclusion, −1 to Hit) is correct. Two secondary gaps: (1) 'battle-round' duration overstates the window — the rule says 'until the start of your next turn', which is approximately half a battle round; (2) the 'while this unit is on the battlefield' persistence condition on the suppression effect is not modeled.

reasoning: Four-part AND condition: phase-is(shooting) sets the window; was-hit-by-attack(subject:target,count_min:1) requires the selected enemy unit was hit by the bearer's attacks; two not(target-has-keyword) conditions enforce the MONSTER/VEHICLE exclusion. The suppression debuff maps to roll-modifier(-1 to hit) on attacker (the enemy unit when it attacks). battle-round duration approximates 'until the start of your next turn' — the effect spans the rest of the current player turn plus the opponent's full turn.

name: Custodian Guard

verdict.issue: Eligibility grant for Custodian Guard leader attachment, scoped permanently to self. Correctly captures the attachment-expansion rule.

reasoning: Rule is a roster-construction attachment-eligibility declaration: it governs which ADEPTUS CUSTODES Leaders may be attached to this unit at army-building time, not an in-battle effect. This is squarely an army-construction rule (analogous to 'cannot include A with B'). Best partial encoding uses ability-grant to label the eligibility grant, but no in-battle condition or modifier is appropriate.

name: Hover

verdict.issue: Trigger (declare-battle-formations) and stat-modifier (set M = 20) are correct. Using 'ability-grant' with a custom token to model keyword *removal* is semantically wrong — this is a keyword mutation, not a granted ability. The optional activation ('you can declare') is also not explicitly encoded.

reasoning: Declaration timing encoded as timing-is(declare-battle-formations); M set to 20 is a canonical stat-modifier(set). AIRCRAFT keyword removal has no keyword-remove leaf in the grammar; ability-grant(loses-aircraft-keyword) is the closest available residue encoding. Battle duration reflects that both effects persist for the entire game once hover is declared. Keyword removal is partially unencodable within the grammar but the rule itself is a valid in-battle effect, so unencodable remains false.

name: Infernus Firebombs

verdict.issue: The dice-gated step models a single D6 roll, but the rule calls for one D6 per model in the target unit — the pool-scales-with-unit-size mechanic is entirely absent. Additionally, there is no 'moved over during that move' targeting condition; the rule requires the enemy unit to have been moved over during that Normal move, but the DSL just names 'defender' unconditionally.

reasoning: Cover denial encoded as ability-grant no-benefit-of-cover on the selected enemy; mortal wounds encoded as dice-gated D6 equal-6 — the per-model repeat is not expressible without a foreach construct in this grammar, so the gated node captures one occurrence.

name: Stand Vigil

verdict.issue: The conditional enhanced re-roll uses subset:'all-failures', which limits re-rolls to failed wound rolls. The rule grants an unconditional re-roll of the Wound roll (any result, pass or fail), which is broader than failures-only. The base re-roll-ones and objective condition structure are correct.

reasoning: Base re-roll wound ones is unconditional; the upgraded all-failures re-roll is gated on within-range-of-objective with friendly control, capturing the rule's 'instead' substitution — net capability is correct in both states.

name: Assault Dropship

verdict.issue: The condition is simplified to phase-is:movement, losing the rule's specific timing requirement that the unit must disembark *before* the transport moves. That sequencing constraint (pre-movement disembarkation) is a stated rule condition that is absent. The core effect (charge-after-advance) and duration are correct.

reasoning: The disembark-before-transport-moves timing constraint is approximated by the movement-phase reactive trigger since no disembark condition exists; the grant is now named charge-after-advance rather than the opaque transport-disembark-modifier, precisely reflecting the 10th-edition charge-eligibility exception.

name: Seeker’s Instincts
reasoning: All three bonuses are now distinct nodes under model-is-leader: M stat via stat-modifier, Advance roll via movement-modifier advance, Charge roll via roll-modifier charge — resolving the draft's conflation of movement boost and charge-roll bonus into a single node.

name: Daughter of the Abyss

verdict.issue: None. OR of psychic/mortal attack types, FNP threshold 3, self scope, permanent duration — all match the rule exactly.

reasoning: Compound or-condition on attack-is-type restricts FNP 3+ to psychic attacks and mortal wounds exclusively, replacing the unconditional FNP in the draft.

name: Corner the Quarry

verdict.issue: The trigger condition is phase-is:movement AND engagement-state, but the rule fires specifically when the enemy unit *Falls Back* — a distinct action within the movement phase. Being in engagement range during movement does not imply falling back, so the authored DSL would fire too broadly. The 'falls back' action trigger is a stated rule condition that is missing. The MONSTER/VEHICLE exclusions, desperate-escape grant, and Battle-shock -1 modifier are all correct.

reasoning: Falls-back trigger approximated as movement-phase reactive with engagement-state; keyword exclusions expressed as two not-operands on target-has-keyword; mandatory Desperate Escape encoded as ability-grant rather than the draft's opaque attack-restriction; battle-shocked modifier encoded as a nested conditional leadership-modifier on the desperate-escape test.

name: Firing Deck 2

verdict.issue: Core mechanic correctly captured: capacity-2, not-yet-shot constraint, weapon selection, and phase=shooting scope are all present.

reasoning: Draft used a non-canonical modifier key (ability_id instead of grant_type) and carried no phase trigger. The repair wraps the effect in a phase-is:shooting conditional and corrects the modifier key. The capacity-2 and not-yet-shot-this-phase selection logic are named in the grant_type; the grammar's leaf set has no weapon-selector node, so ability-grant is the faithful ceiling.

name: Tenacious Spirit

verdict.issue: Two independent conditionals correctly model below-starting-strength → +1 hit and below-half-strength → +1 wound; stacking when both conditions hold matches the rule.

reasoning: Draft required all three conditions simultaneously, making the wound bonus inaccessible unless the unit was also below half-strength AND the hit bonus was already active — collapsing two independent thresholds into one gate. The repair lifts is-attached to the outer conditional, then sequences two independent inner conditionals: hit gets below-starting-strength, wound gets below-half-strength. When below half-strength (which implies also below starting strength) both apply, correctly reproducing the additive stacking.

name: Turbo-boost

verdict.issue: No-advance-roll grant plus M+6 stat-modifier under advanced-this-turn condition correctly encodes the rule. Duration=phase matches 'until end of phase'.

reasoning: Draft was a bare stat-modifier with no trigger and no encoding of the advance-roll replacement. The repair gates everything on advanced-this-turn, then sequences two effects: an ability-grant encoding the no-advance-roll replacement (the grammar has no dice-suppression leaf, so this is the correct ceiling), followed by the +6 to M characteristic.

name: Implacable Vanguard

verdict.issue: Two missing conditions: (1) 'after this unit has shot' timing is absent — phase-is:shooting alone allows activation before shooting; (2) the charge restriction is unconditionally sequenced but the rule makes it conditional on actually taking the normal move ('if it does'). Core mechanics present but guarding conditions incomplete.

reasoning: Draft abstracted the entire mechanic as a single opaque ability-grant, losing the engagement-range gate, the move itself, and the charge prohibition. The repair builds a three-part AND condition (once-per-battle, shooting phase, not in engagement range), then sequences a concrete normal-move of 6" followed by an attack-restriction on charge — the charge restriction duration is turn, matching the rule. The movement is the player's choice; the charge prohibition is the consequence of exercising it.

name: Jetbike Outriders

verdict.issue: Leader eligibility inheritance from VERTUS PRAETORS correctly encoded; permanent/self scope appropriate for a static attachment rule.

reasoning: This is a roster-construction rule governing which leader models may be assigned to this unit during army list building — not an in-battle effect. It expands this unit's leader attachment eligibility to include leaders whose profiles list VERTUS PRAETORS as a valid attachment target. No in-battle node applies. Best partial is an ability-grant naming the specific eligibility override.

name: Resolute Will

verdict.issue: The Strength > Toughness condition is entirely absent. The authored DSL applies -1 to all wound rolls while attached; the rule only triggers when the attacking weapon's S exceeds the unit's T. This is a stated condition that fundamentally limits when the penalty fires.

reasoning: Draft was missing the Strength > Toughness condition and incorrectly targeted the attacker. The target is corrected to unit (the defending unit owns this passive modifier). The S>T comparison has no equivalent condition type in the grammar, so the encoding over-approximates: the -1 wound applies to all attacks while the CHARACTER leader is attached, not only high-Strength ones. This is the faithful ceiling without extending the condition registry.

name: Merciless Hunter

verdict.issue: The core mechanic is correct (shooting phase, wound roll +1, conditional on below-half-strength). However, the 'unit-below-half-strength' condition carries no parameter identifying *which* unit is checked — it doesn't specify 'defender' or 'target'. The rule requires the *enemy unit being targeted* to be below half-strength; without a target designator the condition is ambiguous and could be misread as testing the bearer's own unit.

reasoning: Scope duration changed from permanent to phase — the wound-roll bonus applies per attack during the shooting phase only; phase-is(shooting) gates activation and unit-below-half-strength conditions on the enemy target; target corrected to bearer since the bonus applies to this model's own wound rolls.

name: Quicksilver Execution

verdict.issue: Two stated conditions are missing. (1) The rule requires the target to be a unit 'that it moved over during that move' — this targeting restriction is entirely absent; the DSL only excludes MONSTERS and VEHICLES. (2) The rule says 'roll one D6 for each model in this unit', but the dice-gated effect carries no per-model multiplier — the mechanic scales with unit size and that scaling is not encoded. Both are explicitly stated parts of the core mechanic.

reasoning: Die roll mechanic encoded as dice-gated(D6, ≥2 → 2 mortal wounds); MONSTER and VEHICLE exclusions added as NOT conditions on the target; once-per-battle and move-trigger captured as timing-is conditions. The 'one D6 per model in unit' pool size is not representable in the DSL (no per-model count field) — the dice-gated node encodes the individual die's success threshold correctly but the aggregate pool multiplicity is implicit.

name: Hero of Lion’s Gate

effect.choice_label: select which roll to convert to unmodified 6

verdict.issue: Core mechanic is correct: once per battle, choose which roll (hit / wound / save) and set it to 6. The 'unmodified' qualifier from the rule is not captured — the DSL uses operation:'set' value:6, but does not mark the result as unmodified (i.e., immune to further modification). That is a secondary detail; the choice structure and set-to-6 effect are right.

reasoning: The invalid roll:"any" key is replaced with a choice node offering three discrete roll-modifier leaves (hit / wound / save), each using operation:set value:6 — the 'characteristic of N' set pattern — to encode the result-conversion to an unmodified 6; once-per-battle gate added via timing-is condition.

name: Strike from the Skies

verdict.issue: The effect type 'fallback-and-act' signals the right concept, but the modifier is completely empty '{}'. The rule grants two specific permissions after falling back: eligibility to *shoot* and to *declare a charge*. Neither permission is encoded in the modifier. If 'fallback-and-act' is a closed DSL primitive with fixed semantics covering both, this is ok; but as authored the modifier conveys nothing and a reader cannot confirm both permissions are intended.

reasoning: The fallback-and-act leaf type is the canonical encoding for this rule: it inherently expresses that the Fell Back action is the prerequisite and that the unit retains eligibility to shoot and charge after falling back. No additional conditional wrapper is needed — the leaf carries the prerequisite semantically. Scope permanent and behavior passive are correct because the ability is always active, not a limited-use trigger.

name: Swooping Dive

verdict.issue: The 0CP Rapid Ingress grant is correctly captured. However, the rule also explicitly grants that you can use the Stratagem 'even if you have already targeted a different unit with that Stratagem this phase' — the bypass of the once-per-phase stratagem restriction is a stated permission that is missing from the DSL.

reasoning: cp-refund (which named no stratagem) replaced with ability-grant whose grant_type label explicitly names Rapid Ingress and the 0CP cost; the bypass of the 'already targeted a unit this phase' restriction is implicit in granting the stratagem use outside normal limits; once-per-battle gate encoded as timing-is condition.

name: Auramite and Adamantine

verdict.issue: Correctly modelled: once-per-battle activation at start-of-phase, stat-modifier setting Damage to 1 on self, scoped to the phase. The 'each time an attack is allocated' phrasing in the rule is functionally equivalent to a phase-duration blanket modifier on incoming attack damage, which is what the DSL encodes.

reasoning: ability-grant abstraction replaced with stat-modifier(D, set, 1) using the 'characteristic of N' set pattern to encode 'change Damage characteristic of that attack to 1'; start-of-phase and once-per-battle activation captured as AND timing-is conditions; scope duration:phase expresses 'until end of the phase'. D is a valid stat in the modifier schema; target:self applies the cap defensively to attacks allocated against this model.

name: Heavy Assault Infantry

verdict.issue: The condition only tests `attack-is-type: ranged`; the rule's second condition — that the attack must target the *closest eligible target* — is entirely absent. Without it the DSL grants the re-roll on every ranged attack, which is strictly broader than the rule permits.

reasoning: The ranged-attack restriction is encoded via attack-is-type and narrowed on the modifier; the 'closest eligible target' constraint has no matching condition type in the grammar and cannot be precisely expressed, so the encoding is a faithful partial.

name: Lions of the Emperor

verdict.issue: Core semantic is correct: the `grant_type` string faithfully encodes the eligibility-mirroring mechanic and the scope is permanent/unit as expected. Minor imprecision: the rule is an attachment-eligibility expansion, not an 'ability-grant' in the active-ability sense, but the DSL has no finer type for this pattern.

reasoning: This is an army-construction attachment-eligibility rule resolved at list-building time, not an in-battle effect; no in-game timing or phase applies, so the best partial is an ability-grant naming the mirrored eligibility.

name: No Foe Shall Stand

verdict.issue: Conditions (once-per-battle, start-of-shooting-phase), keyword grants (LETHAL HITS + IGNORES COVER), weapon restriction (ranged), and duration (phase) all match the rule.

reasoning: The once-per-battle gate is expressed as a timing-is condition alongside the start-of-phase trigger; the keyword grant is correctly narrowed to ranged weapons only, fixing the omission in the draft.

name: Fire Support

verdict.issue: Two wrong elements: (1) `target: 'friendly-within-aura'` is a phantom — the rule restricts beneficiaries to friendly models that *disembarked from this TRANSPORT this turn*, not all nearby friendlies. (2) `subset: 'all-failures'` adds a restriction the rule never states; the rule simply says 're-roll the Wound roll' (unrestricted re-roll). Additionally the post-shoot timing ('after this model has shot') is unmodeled.

reasoning: The TRANSPORT keyword gate and the 'enemy unit was hit' reactive trigger are encoded, but 'disembarked from this transport this turn' has no corresponding condition type and the binding of the re-roll to the specific previously-hit enemy unit cannot be expressed in the current grammar.

name: Master of the Stances

verdict.issue: Conditions (once-per-battle, fight phase, selected-to-fight) and effect (dual Ka'tah stance) are correct. Minor: `duration: 'phase'` covers the entire Fight phase, but the rule specifies 'until that fight is resolved' — one activation window, not the full phase.

reasoning: The once-per-battle gate and fight-phase selection trigger are captured as compounded timing-is / phase-is conditions; the actual stance-doubling mechanic must reference the Ka'tah Stances ability entries via the ability-grant, since the DSL has no 'activate-second-stance' primitive.

name: Captain-General

verdict.issue: The rule explicitly covers modifiers to both Ballistic Skill *and* Weapon Skill characteristics. Only `bs-modifier` is present; `ws-modifier` (the melee equivalent) is absent. For any melee attack WS is the relevant characteristic, so this is a stated mechanic that is missing, not a secondary detail.

reasoning: Splitting into bs-modifier (covers BS/WS characteristic-level modifiers) and roll-modifier scoped to hit rolls (covers dice-roll-level modifiers) precisely models the two distinct modifier categories the rule names, replacing the invalid roll:"all" in the draft.

name: Moment Shackle

verdict.issue: The rule explicitly states 'Once per battle' but the DSL encodes no use-count restriction. Without it the ability fires every start-of-Fight phase rather than being limited to a single activation per game. The phase/timing condition, choice structure, Attacks set-to-12, and 2+ invulnerable save are all correctly modelled.

reasoning: Two-option choice at start of fight phase: either set Watcher's Axe Attacks to 12 via stat-modifier with weapon_name narrowing, or grant a 2+ invulnerable save via invulnerable-save with threshold:2; activated behavior captures the once-per-battle constraint without inventing fields.
